steps.name: Shared foundation: schema, db, dietary, types
criteria: createDb returns a live DbClient with WAL mode enabled and probe passes
discountItems, mealPlans, savingsLog, scrapeJobs tables defined in schema
isCompatible returns true/false per dietary restriction and tag set
steps.implementation_notes: Implement Drizzle table definitions in schema.ts (sqliteTable, text, integer columns for all tables). createDb opens better-sqlite3 in WAL mode and runs write-read-delete probe before returning — throw on probe failure. isCompatible is a pure predicate: no I/O, no side-effects. TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests using fast-check @given strategies + state-delta matchers. Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only — document why in a `# bypass:` comment if used. Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example — they verify wiring, not invariants.

steps.name: Scraping pipeline and discount registration
criteria: FakeAldiCatalogueAdapter.fromFixtureFile reads fixture JSON and fetchCurrentWeek returns items
CatalogueNormalizer.normalize filters items missing both price and discountedPrice (D21)
CatalogueNormalizer.normalize maps price strings to integer cents and applies dietaryTags
scraper-runner exits 0 when CATALOGUE_SOURCE=fake and fixture contains valid items
Normalised items are stored via DiscountService.registerDiscountItem
steps.scenario_name: Walking Skeleton — Scenario 1: Shopper sees discounted items, generates a meal plan, and confirms savings match the estimate
steps.implementation_notes: ScrapingService.run orchestrates: start job, fetch, normalize, registerDiscountItem for each item, complete job. scraper-runner reads CATALOGUE_SOURCE env var to select adapter: FakeAldiCatalogueAdapter.fromFixtureFile(FAKE_CATALOGUE_FIXTURE) or live fetcher. CatalogueNormalizer contract shape: pure-function / return-only — no DB writes, no I/O. DiscountService.registerDiscountItem is the only impure function in this boundary (writes to sqlite_discount_item_repository). D21: normalizer MUST drop items where discountedPrice is absent or empty. D22: regularPrice is written once; the repository must not allow updates. TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests using fast-check @given strategies + state-delta matchers. Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only — document why in a `# bypass:` comment if used. Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example — they verify wiring, not invariants.

steps.name: Discount HTTP feed: GET /
criteria: GET / returns 200 HTML containing item names and both regular and sale prices
Items missing either price are absent from the feed (D21 filter visible in output)
steps.scenario_name: Walking Skeleton — Scenario 1: Shopper sees discounted items, generates a meal plan, and confirms savings match the estimate
steps.implementation_notes: DiscountHandler.handleGet calls DiscountService.getWeeklyItems and renders HTMX HTML with item name, regular price (was €X.XX), and sale price. Handler is a thin driving adapter — no business logic, no DB access directly. Contract shape: bounded-change (reads discount_items, no writes). TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests using fast-check @given strategies + state-delta matchers. Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only — document why in a `# bypass:` comment if used. Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example — they verify wiring, not invariants.

steps.name: Plan generation and savings tracking (D23 atomic)
criteria: POST /plan/generate returns 200 and GET /plan renders data-estimated-savings attribute in cents
GET /savings renders data-saved-amount attribute equal to data-estimated-savings (D23)
PlanService.savePlan writes meal_plans and savings_log in a single SQLite transaction
steps.scenario_name: Walking Skeleton — Scenario 1: Shopper sees discounted items, generates a meal plan, and confirms savings match the estimate
steps.implementation_notes: PlanService.generatePlan is a pure function (plan-value pattern): takes weekStart + prefs, reads discount items via DiscountService, returns a MealPlan value — no DB writes. PlanService.savePlan is the ONLY impure function: opens a single SQLite transaction, writes meal_plans.estimated_savings and savings_log.saved_amount atomically — the D23 invariant means partial writes are structurally impossible. plan-handler renders estimated_savings as `data-estimated-savings="{cents}"`. savings-handler renders saved_amount as `data-saved-amount="{cents}"`. SavingsService.recordSavings is called by PlanService.savePlan inside the same tx — NOT via HTTP. TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests using fast-check @given strategies + state-delta matchers. Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only — document why in a `# bypass:` comment if used. Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example — they verify wiring, not invariants.

steps.name: Server composition root (D35 wire order)
criteria: createServer wires all services and routes in order: createDb, services, probe, routes
Server starts and acceptance test suite passes green end-to-end
steps.scenario_name: Walking Skeleton — Scenario 1: Shopper sees discounted items, generates a meal plan, and confirms savings match the estimate
steps.implementation_notes: createServer follows D35 wire order: (1) createDb(dbPath), (2) instantiate all SQLite adapters and services, (3) run probe — emit health.startup.refused and exit 1 on failure, (4) register routes: GET / → DiscountHandler, POST /plan/generate and GET /plan → PlanHandler, GET /savings → SavingsHandler. Returns ServerHandle with stop(). When CATALOGUE_SOURCE=fake the server does not wire the scraping pipeline — scraper-runner.ts is the separate entry point. TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Unit tests for this step MUST be written as property-based tests using fast-check @given strategies + state-delta matchers. Single-example unit tests are FALLBACK only — document why in a `# bypass:` comment if used. Integration and E2E tests REMAIN single-example — they verify wiring, not invariants.
